  "text": "The Grafana datasource plugin query path consists of three main layers:\n\n## 1. HTTP API Entry Points\n\n**pkg/api/ds_query.go** - Legacy HTTP API endpoint that routes datasource queries. The getDSQueryEndpoint() handler detects feature flags to either route to the legacy path or the new query service API.\n\n**pkg/registry/apis/query/query.go** - Kubernetes-style REST API layer implementing the K8s API server pattern. The queryREST struct implements the Connect() method which returns an HTTP handler that processes POST requests to execute queries. This is the primary entry point for the new query service.\n\n## 2. Query Request Handler and Dispatch\n\n**pkg/registry/apis/query/query.go** - The handleQuery() function processes incoming QueryDataRequest objects, extracts headers, and routes to the appropriate handler. The prepareQuery() function normalizes datasource references and builds the MetricRequest used by the legacy query service. The handlePreparedQuery() function invokes the legacy query service.\n\n**pkg/services/query/query.go** - The ServiceImpl implements the core query execution logic. Key methods:\n- queryData() - Routes queries based on whether they contain expressions or target single/multiple datasources\n- handleQuerySingleDatasource() - For single datasource queries, creates a backend.QueryDataRequest and dispatches to either the legacy plugin client (single-tenant) or the query-service client (multi-tenant) via ConvertBackendRequestToDataRequest()\n- handleExpressions() - For expression queries, delegates to the expression service's TransformData()\n- executeConcurrentQueries() - Handles multiple datasource queries concurrently and aggregates responses\n\n## 3. Plugin Backend Query Dispatch\n\n**pkg/plugins/manager/client/client.go** - The Service.QueryData() method looks up the plugin from the registry and invokes its QueryData handler. It handles error wrapping, request cancellation, and ensures frame RefIDs are set from response RefIDs.\n\n**pkg/plugins/backendplugin/coreplugin/core_plugin.go** - Core plugin implementation that wraps backend plugin handlers and invokes them with proper Grafana config context.\n\n**pkg/aggregator/apiserver/plugin/query.go** - The QueryDataHandler() processes query requests in the aggregator layer (for multi-tenant deployments). It validates datasource references, obtains plugin context, and dispatches to the plugin client.\n\n**pkg/aggregator/apiserver/plugin/handler.go** - The PluginHandler routes requests to appropriate service handlers based on the aggregation service specification. For QueryServiceType, it routes to the QueryDataHandler().\n\n**pkg/registry/apis/datasource/sub_query.go** - Sub-resource query handler for direct datasource querying. The Connect() method returns an HTTP handler that binds the QueryDataRequest, extracts queries, and dispatches to the plugin client.\n\n## 4. Expression and Query Transformation\n\n**pkg/expr/service.go** - The Service.TransformData() method processes expression pipelines. It executes the expression graph using the datasource clients to fetch data and transform it according to the expression configuration.\n\n**pkg/expr/query_convert.go** - ConvertBackendRequestToDataRequest() converts backend.QueryDataRequest to the K8s-style data.QueryDataRequest format used by the query-service client. This enables single datasource queries in multi-tenant scenarios.\n\n## 5. Response Transformation and Marshaling\n\n**pkg/apis/datasource/v0alpha1/query.go** - Defines the QueryDataResponse wrapper type that embeds backend.QueryDataResponse and adds Kubernetes metadata. The GetResponseCode() helper function determines the appropriate HTTP status code based on response content.\n\n**pkg/registry/apis/query/query.go** - In the Connect() method's handler, the responder.Object() calls wrap the backend.QueryDataResponse in a query.QueryDataResponse before serialization. Error handling converts errors to appropriate error responses with proper status codes and error sources.\n\n**pkg/services/pluginsintegration/clientmiddleware/** - Middleware chain that wraps the plugin client:\n- caching_middleware.go - Caches query results when applicable\n- httpclient_middleware.go - Sets HTTP headers on plugin requests\n- metrics_middleware.go - Records request metrics\n- tracing_middleware.go - Adds distributed tracing spans\n\nThese middleware components intercept QueryData calls to add cross-cutting concerns before dispatching to the underlying plugin implementation.\n\n## Complete Query Path Chain\n\n1. HTTP request arrives at getDSQueryEndpoint() or queryREST.Connect()\n2. Request is routed to queryREST.Connect() for the new query service\n3. handleQuery() parses and prepares the request\n4. handlePreparedQuery() invokes service.QueryData()\n5. ServiceImpl.queryData() routes based on expression vs datasource queries\n6. For datasources: handleQuerySingleDatasource() builds backend.QueryDataRequest\n7. Single-tenant: pluginClient.QueryData() → plugin backend execution\n8. Multi-tenant: ConvertBackendRequestToDataRequest() → query-service client → aggregator plugin query handler\n9. Plugin middleware stack processes the request\n10. Plugin backend executes QueryData and returns QueryDataResponse\n11. Response is wrapped in v0alpha1.QueryDataResponse with proper HTTP status codes\n12. Responder serializes and sends the response\n\nFor expression queries, the path diverges to TransformData() which internally uses the datasource clients to fetch data and apply transformations."

5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

20: func (h *PluginHandler) QueryDataHandler() http.HandlerFunc {
21: 	return func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {
22: 		ctx := req.Context()
23: 		span := tracing.SpanFromContext(ctx)
24: 		span.AddEvent("QueryDataHandler")
25: 		responder := &util.Responder{ResponseWriter: w}
26: 		dqr := data.QueryDataRequest{}
27: 		if err := json.NewDecoder(req.Body).Decode(&dqr); err != nil {
28: 			responder.Error(w, req, err)
29: 			return
30: 		}
31: 
32: 		dsUID := req.PathValue("uid")
33: 		dsType := h.dataplaneService.Spec.PluginID
34: 
35: 		queries, dsRef, err := data.ToDataSourceQueries(dqr)
36: 		if err != nil {
37: 			responder.Error(w, req, err)
38: 			return
39: 		}
40: 
41: 		// this shouldn't happen, but just in case
42: 		if dsRef == nil {
43: 			responder.Error(w, req, fmt.Errorf("missing datasource reference"))
44: 			return
45: 		}
46: 
47: 		// the datasource type in the query body should match the plugin ID
48: 		if dsRef.Type != dsType {
49: 			err := errors.New("invalid datasource type")
50: 			klog.ErrorS(err, err.Error(), "dsType", dsType, "refType", dsRef.Type)
51: 			responder.Error(w, req, err)
52: 			return
53: 		}
54: 
55: 		// the UID in the query body should match the UID in the URL
56: 		if dsRef.UID != dsUID {
57: 			err := errors.New("invalid datasource UID")
58: 			klog.ErrorS(err, err.Error(), "path", dsUID, "refUID", dsRef.UID)
59: 			responder.Error(w, req, err)
60: 			return
61: 		}
62: 
63: 		span.AddEvent("GetPluginContext",
64: 			grafanasemconv.GrafanaDatasourceUid(dsRef.UID),
65: 			grafanasemconv.GrafanaDatasourceType(dsRef.Type),
66: 		)
67: 		pluginContext, err := h.pluginContextProvider.GetPluginContext(ctx, dsRef.Type, dsRef.UID)
68: 		if err != nil {
69: 			responder.Error(w, req, fmt.Errorf("unable to get plugin context: %w", err))
70: 			return
71: 		}
72: 
73: 		ctx = backend.WithGrafanaConfig(ctx, pluginContext.GrafanaConfig)
74: 		span.AddEvent("QueryData start", grafanasemconv.GrafanaDatasourceRequestQueryCount(len(queries)))
75: 		rsp, err := h.client.QueryData(ctx, &backend.QueryDataRequest{
76: 			Queries:       queries,
77: 			PluginContext: pluginContext,
78: 		})
79: 		if err != nil {
80: 			responder.Error(w, req, err)
81: 			return
82: 		}
83: 		statusCode := 200
84: 		span.AddEvent("QueryData end", semconv.HTTPResponseStatusCode(statusCode))
85: 		responder.Object(statusCode,
86: 			&aggregationv0alpha1.QueryDataResponse{QueryDataResponse: *rsp},
87: 		)
88: 	}
89: }

261: type preparedQuery struct {
262: 	mReq          dtos.MetricRequest
263: 	cache         datasources.CacheService
264: 	headers       map[string]string
265: 	logger        log.Logger
266: 	builder       dsquerierclient.QSDatasourceClientBuilder
267: 	exprSvc       *expr.Service
268: 	reportMetrics func()

271: func prepareQuery(
272: 	ctx context.Context,
273: 	raw query.QueryDataRequest,
274: 	b QueryAPIBuilder,
275: 	httpreq *http.Request,
276: 	connectLogger log.Logger,
277: ) (*preparedQuery, error) {
278: 	// Normalize DS refs and build []*simplejson.Json
279: 	jsonQueries := make([]*simplejson.Json, 0, len(raw.Queries))
280: 	for _, q := range raw.Queries {
281: 		if dsRef, derr := getValidDataSourceRef(ctx, q.Datasource, q.DatasourceID, b.legacyDatasourceLookup); derr != nil {
282: 			connectLogger.Error("error getting valid datasource ref", "err", derr)
283: 		} else if dsRef != nil {
284: 			q.Datasource = dsRef
285: 		}
286: 
287: 		jsonBytes, err := json.Marshal(q)
288: 		if err != nil {
289: 			connectLogger.Error("error marshalling query", "err", err)
290: 		}
291: 
292: 		sjQuery, err := simplejson.NewJson(jsonBytes)
293: 		if err != nil {
294: 			connectLogger.Error("error creating simplejson for query", "err", err)
295: 		}
296: 
297: 		jsonQueries = append(jsonQueries, sjQuery)
298: 	}
299: 
300: 	mReq := dtos.MetricRequest{
301: 		From:    raw.From,
302: 		To:      raw.To,
303: 		Queries: jsonQueries,
304: 	}
305: 
306: 	cache := &MyCacheService{
307: 		legacy: b.legacyDatasourceLookup,
308: 	}
309: 	headers := ExtractKnownHeaders(httpreq.Header)
310: 
311: 	instance, err := b.instanceProvider.GetInstance(ctx, connectLogger, headers)
312: 	if err != nil {
313: 		connectLogger.Error("failed to get instance configuration settings", "err", err)
314: 		return nil, err
315: 	}
316: 
317: 	instanceConfig := instance.GetSettings()
318: 
319: 	dsQuerierLoggerWithSlug := instance.GetLogger()
320: 
321: 	// Datasource client qsDsClientBuilder
322: 	qsDsClientBuilder := dsquerierclient.NewQsDatasourceClientBuilderWithInstance(
323: 		instance,
324: 		ctx,
325: 		dsQuerierLoggerWithSlug,
326: 	)
327: 
328: 	// Expressions service
329: 	exprService := expr.ProvideService(
330: 		&setting.Cfg{
331: 			ExpressionsEnabled:            instanceConfig.ExpressionsEnabled,
332: 			SQLExpressionCellLimit:        instanceConfig.SQLExpressionCellLimit,
333: 			SQLExpressionOutputCellLimit:  instanceConfig.SQLExpressionOutputCellLimit,
334: 			SQLExpressionTimeout:          instanceConfig.SQLExpressionTimeout,
335: 			SQLExpressionQueryLengthLimit: instanceConfig.SQLExpressionQueryLengthLimit,
336: 		},
337: 		nil,
338: 		nil,
339: 		instanceConfig.FeatureToggles,
340: 		nil,
341: 		b.tracer,
342: 		qsDsClientBuilder,
343: 	)
344: 
345: 	return &preparedQuery{
346: 		mReq:          mReq,
347: 		cache:         cache,
348: 		headers:       headers,
349: 		logger:        dsQuerierLoggerWithSlug,
350: 		builder:       qsDsClientBuilder,
351: 		exprSvc:       exprService,
352: 		reportMetrics: func() { instance.ReportMetrics() },
353: 	}, nil
354: }
355: 
356: func handlePreparedQuery(ctx context.Context, pq *preparedQuery, concurrentQueryLimit int) (*backend.QueryDataResponse, error) {
357: 	resp, err := service.QueryData(ctx, pq.logger, pq.cache, pq.exprSvc, pq.mReq, pq.builder, pq.headers, concurrentQueryLimit)
358: 	pq.reportMetrics()
359: 	return resp, err
360: }
361: 
362: func handleQuery(
363: 	ctx context.Context,
364: 	raw query.QueryDataRequest,
365: 	b QueryAPIBuilder,
366: 	httpreq *http.Request,
367: 	responder responderWrapper,
368: 	connectLogger log.Logger,
369: ) (*backend.QueryDataResponse, error) {
370: 	pq, err := prepareQuery(ctx, raw, b, httpreq, connectLogger)
371: 	if err != nil {
372: 		responder.Error(err)
373: 		return nil, err
374: 	}
375: 	return handlePreparedQuery(ctx, pq, b.concurrentQueryLimit)
376: }

8: 	data "github.com/grafana/grafana-plugin-sdk-go/experimental/apis/datasource/v0alpha1"
9: )

11: func convertBackendQueryToDataQuery(q backend.DataQuery) (data.DataQuery, error) {
12: 	// we first restore it from the raw json data,
13: 	// this should take care of all datasource-specific (for example, specific
14: 	// for prometheus or loki) fields
15: 	var dataQuery data.DataQuery
16: 	err := json.Unmarshal(q.JSON, &dataQuery)
17: 	if err != nil {
18: 		return data.DataQuery{}, err
19: 	}
20: 
21: 	// then we override the result with values that are available as fields in backend.DataQuery
22: 	dataQuery.RefID = q.RefID
23: 	dataQuery.QueryType = q.QueryType
24: 	dataQuery.MaxDataPoints = q.MaxDataPoints
25: 	dataQuery.IntervalMS = float64(q.Interval.Nanoseconds()) / 1000000.0
26: 	dataQuery.TimeRange = &data.TimeRange{
27: 		From: strconv.FormatInt(q.TimeRange.From.UnixMilli(), 10),
28: 		To:   strconv.FormatInt(q.TimeRange.To.UnixMilli(), 10),
29: 	}
30: 
31: 	return dataQuery, nil
32: }
33: 
34: func ConvertBackendRequestToDataRequest(req *backend.QueryDataRequest) (*data.QueryDataRequest, error) {
35: 	k8sReq := &data.QueryDataRequest{
36: 		// `backend.QueryDataRequest` does not have a concept of a top-level global from/to.
37: 		// timeRanges are always inside the queries,
38: 		// so we leave them empty here too.
39: 	}
40: 
41: 	for _, q := range req.Queries {
42: 		dataQuery, err := convertBackendQueryToDataQuery(q)
43: 		if err != nil {
44: 			return nil, err
45: 		}
46: 
47: 		k8sReq.Queries = append(k8sReq.Queries, dataQuery)
48: 	}
49: 
50: 	return k8sReq, nil
51: }

2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"

6: 	"path"
7: 
8: 	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/serializer"
9:

11: 	aggregationv0alpha1 "github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/aggregator/apis/aggregation/v0alpha1"
12: 	"github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/aggregator/apiserver/plugin/admission"
13: )
14: 
15: type PluginClient interface {
16: 	backend.QueryDataHandler
17: 	backend.AdmissionHandler
18: }
19: 
20: type PluginContextProvider interface {
21: 	GetPluginContext(ctx context.Context, pluginID, uid string) (backend.PluginContext, error)
22: }
23: 
24: type PluginHandler struct {
25: 	mux      *http.ServeMux
26: 	delegate http.Handler
27: 
28: 	client                PluginClient
29: 	pluginContextProvider PluginContextProvider
30: 
31: 	dataplaneService aggregationv0alpha1.DataPlaneService
32: 
33: 	admissionCodecs serializer.CodecFactory
34: }
35: 
36: func NewPluginHandler(
37: 	client PluginClient,
38: 	dataplaneService aggregationv0alpha1.DataPlaneService,
39: 	pluginContextProvider PluginContextProvider,
40: 	delegate http.Handler,
41: ) *PluginHandler {
42: 	h := &PluginHandler{
43: 		mux:                   http.NewServeMux(),
44: 		delegate:              delegate,
45: 		client:                client,
46: 		pluginContextProvider: pluginContextProvider,
47: 		dataplaneService:      dataplaneService,
48: 		admissionCodecs:       admission.GetCodecs(),
49: 	}
50: 	h.registerRoutes()
51: 	return h
52: }
53: 
54: func (h *PluginHandler) registerRoutes() {
55: 	proxyPath := proxyPathBuilder(h.dataplaneService.Spec.Group, h.dataplaneService.Spec.Version)
56: 
57: 	for _, service := range h.dataplaneService.Spec.Services {
58: 		switch service.Type {
59: 		case aggregationv0alpha1.AdmissionControlServiceType:
60: 			h.mux.Handle(proxyPath("/admission/mutate"), h.AdmissionMutationHandler())
61: 			h.mux.Handle(proxyPath("/admission/validate"), h.AdmissionValidationHandler())
62: 		case aggregationv0alpha1.ConversionServiceType:
63: 			// TODO: implement in future PR
64: 		case aggregationv0alpha1.DataSourceProxyServiceType:
65: 			// TODO: implement in future PR
66: 		case aggregationv0alpha1.QueryServiceType:
67: 			h.mux.Handle(proxyPath("/namespaces/{namespace}/datasources/{uid}/query"), h.QueryDataHandler())
68: 		case aggregationv0alpha1.RouteServiceType:
69: 			// TODO: implement in future PR
70: 		case aggregationv0alpha1.StreamServiceType:
71: 			// TODO: implement in future PR
72: 		}
73: 	}
74: 
75: 	// fallback to the delegate
76: 	h.mux.Handle("/", h.delegate)
77: }
78: 
79: func (h *PluginHandler) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {
80: 	h.mux.ServeHTTP(w, req)
81: }
82: 
83: func proxyPathBuilder(group, version string) func(string) string {
84: 	return func(suffix string) string {
85: 		return path.Join("/apis", group, version, suffix)
86: 	}
87: }

The getDSQueryEndpoint() handler detects feature flags to either route to the legacy path or the new query service API.\n\n**pkg/registry/apis/query/query.go** - Kubernetes-style REST API layer implementing the K8s API server pattern. The queryREST struct implements the Connect() method which returns an HTTP handler that processes POST requests to execute queries. This is the primary entry point for the new query service.\n\n## 2. Query Request Handler and Dispatch\n\n**pkg/registry/apis/query/query.go** - The handleQuery() function processes incoming QueryDataRequest objects, extracts headers, and routes to the appropriate handler. The prepareQuery() function normalizes datasource references and builds the MetricRequest used by the legacy query service. The handlePreparedQuery() function invokes the legacy query service.\n\n**pkg/services/query/query.go** - The ServiceImpl implements the core query execution logic. Key methods:\n- queryData() - Routes queries based on whether they contain expressions or target single/multiple datasources\n- handleQuerySingleDatasource() - For single datasource queries, creates a backend.QueryDataRequest and dispatches to either the legacy plugin client (single-tenant) or the query-service client (multi-tenant) via ConvertBackendRequestToDataRequest()\n- handleExpressions() - For expression queries, delegates to the expression service's TransformData()\n- executeConcurrentQueries() - Handles multiple datasource queries concurrently and aggregates responses\n\n## 3. Plugin Backend Query Dispatch\n\n**pkg/plugins/manager/client/client.go** - The Service.QueryData() method looks up the plugin from the registry and invokes its QueryData handler. It handles error wrapping, request cancellation, and ensures frame RefIDs are set from response RefIDs.\n\n**pkg/plugins/backendplugin/coreplugin/core_plugin.go** - Core plugin implementation that wraps backend plugin handlers and invokes them with proper Grafana config context.\n\n**pkg/aggregator/apiserver/plugin/query.go** - The QueryDataHandler() processes query requests in the aggregator layer (for multi-tenant deployments). It validates datasource references, obtains plugin context, and dispatches to the plugin client.\n\n**pkg/aggregator/apiserver/plugin/handler.go** - The PluginHandler routes requests to appropriate service handlers based on the aggregation service specification. For QueryServiceType, it routes to the QueryDataHandler().\n\n**pkg/registry/apis/datasource/sub_query.go** - Sub-resource query handler for direct datasource querying. The Connect() method returns an HTTP handler that binds the QueryDataRequest, extracts queries, and dispatches to the plugin client.\n\n## 4. Expression and Query Transformation\n\n**pkg/expr/service.go** - The Service.TransformData() method processes expression pipelines. It executes the expression graph using the datasource clients to fetch data and transform it according to the expression configuration.\n\n**pkg/expr/query_convert.go** - ConvertBackendRequestToDataRequest() converts backend.QueryDataRequest to the K8s-style data.QueryDataRequest format used by the query-service client. This enables single datasource queries in multi-tenant scenarios.\n\n## 5. Response Transformation and Marshaling\n\n**pkg/apis/datasource/v0alpha1/query.go** - Defines the QueryDataResponse wrapper type that embeds backend.QueryDataResponse and adds Kubernetes metadata. The GetResponseCode() helper function determines the appropriate HTTP status code based on response content.\n\n**pkg/registry/apis/query/query.go** - In the Connect() method's handler, the responder.Object() calls wrap the backend.QueryDataResponse in a query.QueryDataResponse before serialization. Error handling converts errors to appropriate error responses with proper status codes and error sources.\n\n**pkg/services/pluginsintegration/clientmiddleware/** - Middleware chain that wraps the plugin client:\n- caching_middleware.go - Caches query results when applicable\n- httpclient_middleware.go - Sets HTTP headers on plugin requests\n- metrics_middleware.go - Records request metrics\n- tracing_middleware.go - Adds distributed tracing spans\n\nThese middleware components intercept QueryData calls to add cross-cutting concerns before dispatching to the underlying plugin implementation.\n\n## Complete Query Path Chain\n\n1. HTTP request arrives at getDSQueryEndpoint() or queryREST.Connect()\n2. Request is routed to queryREST.Connect() for the new query service\n3. handleQuery() parses and prepares the request\n4. handlePreparedQuery() invokes service.QueryData()\n5. ServiceImpl.queryData() routes based on expression vs datasource queries\n6. For datasources: handleQuerySingleDatasource() builds backend.QueryDataRequest\n7. Single-tenant: pluginClient.QueryData() → plugin backend execution\n8. Multi-tenant: ConvertBackendRequestToDataRequest() → query-service client → aggregator plugin query handler\n9. Plugin middleware stack processes the request\n10. Plugin backend executes QueryData and returns QueryDataResponse\n11. Response is wrapped in v0alpha1.QueryDataResponse with proper HTTP status codes\n12. Responder serializes and sends the response\n\nFor expression queries, the path diverges to TransformData() which internally uses the datasource clients to fetch data and apply transformations."
